assertions.expectation: Explains that surge upgrades require extra compute capacity (maxSurge creates NEW nodes before draining old ones)
assertions.reason: Response explains surge nodes can't be created due to quota constraints and shows understanding that surge creates additional nodes

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting maxUnavailable >0 instead of relying on maxSurge (drains first, then creates — uses less quota)
assertions.reason: Multiple solutions show setting maxUnavailable=1 with maxSurge=0, explicitly demonstrating drain-first approach

assertions.expectation: Warns that maxUnavailable >0 means reduced capacity during upgrade — some pods won't have nodes
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'brief unavailability windows' but doesn't explicitly warn about reduced cluster capacity or pods potentially not having nodes during upgrade

assertions.expectation: Suggests temporarily scaling down non-critical deployments to free up quota for surge nodes
assertions.reason: Response suggests scaling down the node pool itself but doesn't mention scaling down non-critical deployments/workloads to free up quota

assertions.expectation: Mentions that reducing maxSurge to 1 minimizes extra quota needed (slower but fits within constraints)
assertions.reason: Solution #2 shows setting maxSurge=1 with explanation that it reduces resource requirements, addressing the quota constraint

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to update surge settings (maxSurge=0, maxUnavailable=1 for zero-extra-capacity mode)
assertions.reason: Provides exact gcloud command with maxSurge=0 and maxUnavailable=1 parameters for zero-extra-capacity upgrade

assertions.expectation: Recommends doing the upgrade in off-peak hours when fewer pods are running
assertions.reason: Response does not mention timing the upgrade during off-peak hours or when fewer pods are running

assertions.expectation: Mentions requesting a temporary quota increase as a faster alternative to a permanent increase
assertions.reason: Response does not suggest requesting a temporary quota increase, only works around the existing constraint
